title: Inventory Src Playable Contract Modules
summary: Src playable contract module inventory for the vanilla parity rebuild. Captures the twelve subdirectories committed under src/playable/, the 153 TypeScript contract files they contain, the size_bytes/line_count/sha256/export_count fingerprint of each committed file, the Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 concept each subdirectory models, and the runtime status of the surface. Confirms that no module outside src/playable/ imports from src/playable/ today, that the simplified launcher entrypoint src/main.ts and the canonical D_DoomLoop orchestrator src/mainLoop.ts both bypass this contract surface, and that 152 of the 153 committed files reference the canonical bun run doom.ts runtime target literal while one file (src/playable/window-host/createBunCompatibleWin32Window.ts) constructs the same literal at runtime via a template join of TARGET_RUNTIME_PROGRAM, TARGET_RUNTIME_SUBCOMMAND, and TARGET_RUNTIME_ENTRY_FILE.
evidence_method: direct local repository audit performed by the inventory lane: ls and git ls-files src/playable/ to enumerate the twelve committed subdirectories and 153 committed TypeScript files, sha256sum and wc -l over each committed file via createHash and readFileSync inside a Bun script, ripgrep over src/ for from '../playable/' and from './playable/' to confirm zero non-playable runtime imports of the contract surface, ripgrep over each src/playable/<subdir>/<file>.ts for the export keyword, ripgrep over src/playable/ for the literal bun run doom.ts to count runtime-target references, and direct read of src/playable/window-host/createBunCompatibleWin32Window.ts to record the one file that constructs the runtime command at runtime.

playable_directory.owner_lane_source: no row in plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md owns src/playable/; the surface is contract/manifest material inherited from prior plan work and is referenced as such by plan_vanilla_parity/README.md line 21

playable_subdirectories.vanilla_concept_modeled: audio runtime contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/i_sound.c, src/s_sound.c, src/i_oplmusic.c, and src/i_winmusic.c
local-distribution and IWAD-discovery launch contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/d_iwad.c and src/d_main.c
D_DoomMain bun entrypoint contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/d_main.c, src/i_system.c, and src/m_argv.c
M_LoadDefaults and M_SaveDefaults configuration contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/m_config.c and src/m_misc.c
demo playback and recording contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/g_game.c (G_PlayDemo, G_RecordDemo) and src/d_net.c
front-end menu and title-screen contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/m_menu.c and src/d_main.c
game-session orchestration contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/g_game.c and src/p_setup.c
input event and ticcmd contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/i_video.c, src/i_input.c, and src/d_event.c
D_DoomLoop and TryRunTics timing contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/d_main.c and src/d_net.c
software-renderer product wiring contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/r_main.c and src/v_video.c
P_ArchivePlayers and P_UnArchivePlayers save/load contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/p_saveg.c and src/m_misc.c
Win32 window-host contract derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/i_video.c and Choco-Doom Windows host wrappers

runtime_status.notes: src/playable/ is a contract/manifest surface, not a runtime path. No module under src/ outside src/playable/ imports from src/playable/, so the contract files are exercised only by the corresponding tests under test/playable/<subdir>/<kebab-case>.test.ts. The simplified launcher src/main.ts and the canonical D_DoomLoop orchestrator src/mainLoop.ts both bypass this surface. 152 of the 153 committed files contain the literal string 'bun run doom.ts' to anchor the canonical runtime target; src/playable/window-host/createBunCompatibleWin32Window.ts builds the same string at runtime via a template literal joining TARGET_RUNTIME_PROGRAM, TARGET_RUNTIME_SUBCOMMAND, and TARGET_RUNTIME_ENTRY_FILE.

test_coverage.notes: Each src/playable/<subdir>/<camelCase>.ts contract file has a matching test/playable/<subdir>/<kebab-case>.test.ts focused test. The acceptance test directory test/playable/acceptance/ holds an additional ten parity-gate tests not covered by the per-file mapping. The 1-to-1 src/test pairing for the 153 contract files is exercised by Bun test, not by any runtime path.

implications: src/playable/ holds 153 committed contract/manifest TypeScript files across twelve subdirectories (audio-product-integration, bun-launch-local-distribution-boundary, bun-runtime-entry-point, config-persistence, demo-replay, front-end-menus, game-session-wiring, input, real-time-main-loop, rendering-product-integration, save-load-playability, window-host) and is not assigned to a lane in plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md, so the inventory lane must treat it as read-only and any future change to the contract surface belongs to the runtime lane that owns the corresponding subsystem (audio, launch, core, ui, render, save, input).

No module under src/ outside src/playable/ imports from src/playable/ today, so the contract surface is exercised exclusively by the focused tests under test/playable/<subdir>/<kebab-case>.test.ts plus the ten acceptance tests under test/playable/acceptance/; downstream lanes must keep that test invocation surface stable until the runtime path is wired to the vanilla bun run doom.ts entrypoint.

The simplified launcher entrypoint src/main.ts and the canonical D_DoomLoop orchestrator src/mainLoop.ts both bypass src/playable/ entirely; the launch lane must not assume the simplified launcher exercises any of the 153 contract files when it replaces the simplified surface with the vanilla bun run doom.ts startup path.

152 of the 153 committed files contain the literal string bun run doom.ts as the canonical runtime target marker; one file (src/playable/window-host/createBunCompatibleWin32Window.ts) constructs the same string at runtime by joining TARGET_RUNTIME_PROGRAM, TARGET_RUNTIME_SUBCOMMAND, and TARGET_RUNTIME_ENTRY_FILE template parts. Lanes that grep for the literal must understand that this one file reaches the same target through composition, not direct literal.

The contract surface depends on twenty-two non-playable runtime modules across src/audio/, src/bootstrap/, src/config/, src/demo/, src/host/, src/input/, src/launcher/, src/mainLoop.ts, src/oracles/, src/render/, src/save/, and src/ui/, plus four node:* built-ins (node:buffer, node:crypto, node:path, node:process). Any rename or reshaping of those twenty-two runtime modules is a cross-lane breaking change that must be coordinated through plan_vanilla_parity/DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.md before src/playable/ is refactored.
